arts.ttl: Monza face setback Matteo Pessina Injured
arts.sum: Monza have received disappointing news ahead of their upcoming match against Torino, as captain Matteo Pessina has been ruled out due to injury. In a press conference, coach Alessandro Nesta revealed that Pessina has suffered a partial tear of the proximal tendon of his hamstring. “He will be out for a while; it will take time for him to recover,” Nesta stated, indicating that the midfielder’s absence could extend into 2025. Pessina’s injury is significant, as he is expected to miss approximately nine matches, eight of which are in Serie A. arts.ttl: “I wouldn’t choose Milan again” – Pietro Pellegri reflects on his career and offers advice to Camarda
arts.sum: Pietro Pellegri, the 22-year-old Empoli striker, recently opened up about his past experiences and current form in an interview with La Repubblica. The former Genoa, Monaco, Milan, and Torino player shared insights into his career journey and offered advice to emerging talents. Addressing his injury-prone reputation, Pellegri explained, “In recent years, I had played little due to physical problems, and I didn’t feel comfortable in France. I underwent two surgeries at Monaco: one for pubalgia and one for the left adductor. I bear the scars, but they don’t hurt as much as the label of being always injured.
